data.description:
## Description

**What**: Deliver four foundational contract files, implement the decision_id format, create the dso:inference-incident-curator agent, and produce the first-run incident corpus with anti-hallucination validation.

**Why**: All other stories (S2-S4) depend on these shared contracts and the corpus. Creates the structural artifacts that make inference-decision bypasses visible at the layer level. This is the walking skeleton — no behavioral changes to existing skills yet.

**Scope**:
- IN: plugins/dso/docs/contracts/decision-id-format.md; plugins/dso/docs/contracts/inference-envelope.md (INFERENCE_CHALLENGE and INFERENCE_SKIP envelope schemas); plugins/dso/docs/contracts/inference-incident-schema.md; META_QUESTION signal contract file; plugins/dso/agents/inference-incident-curator.md (opus tier; keyword scan, extraction, anti-hallucination validation, 5-incident human review attestation); tests/fixtures/inference-incidents/incidents.jsonl (>=20 incidents with verified ticket-ids); tests/fixtures/inference-incidents/holdout.txt (deterministic held-out split via hash(ticket_id) mod 3 == 0)
- OUT: Modifying existing skills (S2, S3); replay measurement (S4); dogfooding (S5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, plugins/dso/docs/contracts/decision-id-format.md exists documenting format <session_id>:<content_hash>, the EXACT session_id sourcing order with ALL fallback steps enumerated (harness session-id file → DSO_SESSION_ID env → generated pv-session-<timestamp_ms>), and SHA-256 content_hash derivation
  ← Satisfies: "Inference challenges surface as ticket comments with structured payload {decision_id, ...}" (SC3)
- When this story is complete, plugins/dso/docs/contracts/inference-envelope.md exists with INFERENCE_CHALLENGE and INFERENCE_SKIP envelope schemas including all required fields
  ← Satisfies: "Every invocation MUST emit a typed envelope, never silence" (SC2)
- When this story is complete, plugins/dso/docs/contracts/inference-incident-schema.md exists conforming to the curator JSONL output format
  ← Satisfies: "Inference-recall validation against an LLM-curated set of historical planning-inference incidents" (SC4)
- When this story is complete, META_QUESTION signal contract is documented with escalation behavior specified (user escalation, NOT REPLAN_ESCALATE)
  ← Satisfies: "escalate to user via explicit META_QUESTION signal — do NOT REPLAN_ESCALATE" (SC1)
- When this story is complete, plugins/dso/agents/inference-incident-curator.md exists with opus tier designation, corpus scan logic, and anti-hallucination validation requirements
  ← Satisfies: "dso:inference-incident-curator at plugins/dso/agents/inference-incident-curator.md (opus tier)" (SC4)
- When this story is complete, tests/fixtures/inference-incidents/incidents.jsonl has >=20 incidents each with a real ticket-id verified by ticket-exists CLI; holdout.txt contains deterministic held-out split
  ← Satisfies: "Curator targets >=20 incidents on first run" (SC4)
- When this story is complete, a 5-incident human review attestation comment exists on the inference-incident-schema contract doc from a non-curator author
  ← Satisfies: "5-incident deterministic random sample requires human review attestation" (SC4)
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Human review attestation for 5-incident sample is a blocking external dependency — implementation plan must account for this as a manual step
- [Performance] Curator agent keyword scan must use the ticket list CLI with appropriate filters and pagination — NOT raw filesystem scans; bypassing the CLI risks reading uncompacted event streams or archived tickets inconsistently
- [Maintainability] decision-id-format.md must specify the EXACT session_id sourcing fallback chain; S3 implements decision_id emission and will produce divergent IDs across replay (S4) and live emission if the fallback chain is ambiguous

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a planning infrastructure maintainer, I have foundational contracts for inference decisions, decision_id format, and a curated incident corpus so downstream stories can implement consistent decision tracking
